steps.title: Identify the ownership structure signaled in the context
steps.content: The phrase “each genre's data” emphasizes that rock and rap have separate, individual data. This tells us we're describing distinct chorus-to-verse ratios—rock's ratio and rap's ratio—not a single shared ratio.

steps.title: Apply the independent possessive rule
steps.content: When multiple nouns joined by and each independently possess something, both must be possessive. Since “each genre's data” signals independent possession, both rock and rap must take the form rock's and rap's. Choices A (no possessives), C (mixed forms), and D (possessive only on the second noun) violate this rule. That matches choice B — B is correct.
